seoTitle: Tom Price vs Tonebridge — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tom Price and Tonebridge. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Tom Price vs Tonebridge: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tom Price and Tonebridge.
heading: Tom Price vs Tonebridge — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tom Price, Western Australia and Tonebridge, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tom Price has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Tonebridge (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tom Price or Tonebridge safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tom Price has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Tonebridge scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tom Price scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Tonebridge scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tom Price or Tonebridge?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tom Price has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
